microbench_note: batch=256 is 210x cheaper/pos than batch=1; after batching, pure-Python legal_moves (0.199ms) is the residual wall
engine_fast_path: legal_moves: empty-neighbour + no-enemy points skip the capture/suicide floodfill, gated by a cheap superko-hash check (a non-capturing move CAN still hit superko)
equivalence_test: test_engine_fast.py: 460/460 positions match brute-force legal_moves across 3^3/4^3/5^3
crossval_after_changes: 60/60 vs TS reference on both 3^3 and 4^3 fixtures
lesson: caching self.grid in a local broke correctness: _is_legal rebinds self.grid to a fresh snapshot, leaving the cached ref stale/dirty — caught by the equivalence test before trusting the engine
